text: Hi Mum, it's me. I dropped my phone and this is my new number. I'm in trouble and need £500 urgently, can you send it to this account? Don't call the old number.
analysis: Impersonates a family member with a new number, requests urgent money and discourages verification — classic grandparent/family impersonation scam

text: HMRC: You owe £1,240 in unpaid tax. Failure to call 0800-XXX-XXXX within 24 hours will result in your arrest. Press 1 to speak to an officer.
analysis: Impersonates HMRC using threats of arrest and urgent deadlines to pressure victims into calling a fraudulent number and paying fake tax debts

text: Your bank has detected suspicious activity on your account. Please call us immediately on 0800-XXX-XXXX to verify your identity and protect your funds.
analysis: Impersonates a bank, creates urgency about account security to get victims to call a fake number and hand over account details or move money to a 'safe account'

text: Royal Mail: Your parcel is waiting. A customs fee of £2.99 is due before delivery. Pay here: [link] within 48 hours or your parcel will be returned.
analysis: Impersonates Royal Mail or courier services, small fee request leads to a phishing page that steals card details

text: Congratulations! You have been selected for a £250,000 prize. To claim, please call our claims line and pay a small processing fee of £49.99.
analysis: Fake lottery win requiring an upfront fee — once paid, further fees are demanded and no prize exists

text: URGENT: Your Windows computer has a virus. Call Microsoft Support on 0800-XXX-XXXX immediately to prevent your files and banking details from being stolen.
analysis: Impersonates Microsoft or tech support, aims to get remote access to the victim's computer to steal banking credentials or demand payment for fake repairs

text: NHS: Your free annual health check is overdue. Click here to book, and confirm your NHS number and date of birth to complete your registration.
analysis: Impersonates NHS to collect personal details (NHS number, date of birth) used for identity theft or further scams

text: I'm an engineer working overseas. I came across your profile and would love to connect. I also have an investment opportunity with guaranteed 20% returns.
analysis: Builds a fake romantic relationship before introducing a fraudulent investment platform — victims lose large sums before realising the platform is fake

text: You may be entitled to a council tax refund of £312. Complete your claim at this link within 7 days. You'll need your bank sort code and account number.
analysis: Fake government refund offer used to harvest bank account details for fraud

text: Hi, it's your daughter. I'm using a friend's phone. I've been in an accident abroad and need £1,000 wired urgently. Please don't worry Mum, just send it.
analysis: Urgent family emergency story told from an unknown number to prevent verification — a variation of the grandparent scam targeting parents of adult children

text: Your pension can be accessed early, tax-free. Our advisers have helped hundreds unlock their pension before 55. Call now for a free consultation.
analysis: Pension liberation fraud — promises early, tax-free pension access which is illegal; victims face large tax penalties and lose their pension savings

text: This natural supplement can reverse type 2 diabetes, lower blood pressure and cholesterol without medication. Thousands of doctors recommend it secretly.
analysis: False health claims targeting people with chronic conditions, the phrase 'doctors recommend it secretly' is a common manipulation tactic
